changes.reason: Dormant pin-avoid attribution split. Add exactly one dormant charm/pin concept to the VALID_0DTE router so the next run can test whether existing charm_pin_pullback_long and charm_wall_breakaway_long tail heat belongs to a separate router state rather than another broad veto or profile tightening. Full replacement preserves all currently routed VALID_0DTE profiles.

Enable only the dormant charm_wall_pin_avoid_long profile for attribution. Current charm-pin/wall profiles are profitable but tail-heavy: charm_pin_pullback_long owns the -513.4 worst MAE and charm_wall_breakaway_long has small-sample convex PnL with -346.8 worst MAE. This tests whether the built-in pin-avoid concept captures those states without altering their existing thresholds.

recommendation.expected_effect: This should produce either a clean standalone charm_wall_pin_avoid_long sample or show that the dormant router concept is unreachable. Success is not raw trade count; it is positive standalone PnL or lower combined charm-pin/charm-wall MAE while retaining at least 98% of leader net profit, no slow >=12h trades, and protected fast/extended-VWAP winners. Main risk is router cannibalization of profitable charm_pin_pullback_long or charm_wall_breakaway_long entries.
recommendation.label: charm-wall-pin-avoid-router-probe
recommendation.rationale: The next highest-value experiment is attribution, not another global filter. Broad RSI/ADX/VWAP pruning would block too many protected winners, direct 1DTE tightening already hurt, and the latest call-wall sample-builder proposal was rejected as a duplicate. The strategist explicitly prioritizes one dormant pin-avoid activation at a time; this is a two-change, config-only router probe that leaves VALID_0DTE option-confirmed logic and no-0DTE routing intact.

strategy_hypotheses.evidence: charm_pin_pullback_long contributes $1200.3 over 32 VALID_0DTE trades with median hold near 5 minutes, but owns the global -513.4 MAE. charm_wall_breakaway_long contributes $985.6 from only 5 trades at $234.4/hour but still has -346.8 worst MAE. Static filters overlap protected extended-VWAP winners, so a router attribution split is cleaner.
strategy_hypotheses.name: Charm Wall Pin-Avoid Attribution Split
strategy_hypotheses.risk: The dormant profile may be unreachable, or it may cannibalize profitable charm-pin/charm-wall winners without reducing MAE. Reject if total net profit drops below 98% of the leader/base or protected winner retention deteriorates.
strategy_hypotheses.trigger: VALID_0DTE charm-wall or charm-pin proximity state where existing charm_pin_pullback_long or charm_wall_breakaway_long would otherwise absorb pin-risk/tail behavior.
strategy_hypotheses.validation_metric: Activated charm_wall_pin_avoid_long has positive standalone PnL or combined charm-pin/charm-wall worst MAE improves, total net profit stays >=98% of leader/base, completed trades do not fall more than 10%, and slow_12h remains 0.

strategy_hypotheses.evidence: The worst charm_pin_pullback_long tail reached meaningful profit only after about 80 minutes, while protected fast/extended-VWAP winners usually resolve quickly. Config-only static gates have either gone flat or risk blocking protected winners.
strategy_hypotheses.name: Charm Pin Early-Progress Tail Classifier
strategy_hypotheses.risk: Path-aware logic can overfit delayed green recoveries unless validated against protected fast-winner UTCs and out-of-sample days.
strategy_hypotheses.trigger: charm_pin_pullback_long high-extension or recent-high-rejection entries that fail to make early positive progress before MAE.
strategy_hypotheses.validation_metric: Reduce charm_pin_pullback_long worst MAE toward <= -403 while retaining >=90% charm-pin PnL, >=95% protected fast winners, total net profit >=98%, and no slow trades.
